Amharic initial ጸ reflects PS *ṣ, but Ge'ez is ጸፈ (root ጸ-ፈ-ፈ?), showing historical complex shifts in Ethio-Semitic; distinct from the standard *k-t-b root.
cognates.notes: While historically debated, many scholars link the Ethio-Semitic *ṣ-f root to an old *k-t-b metathesis or distinct origin, though cognate status is often treated with caution in handbooks.

caveats: v0 prototype: this is an LLM-generated analysis, not a lexicon lookup. Results may be incomplete or incorrect, especially for Syriac, Tigrinya, and rare forms. Verify against Wiktionary or a scholarly lexicon before citing.
The input 'הכתבתי' is the Hif'il first-person past of the root כ-ת-ב, meaning 'I dictated' or 'I caused (something) to be written'.
